input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-07T14:22:48.215Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are found, whereas VSTest treated that as success.

Let me look at their project files to understand their setup, and also invoke the relevant skill for MTP migration guidance.

toolCalls.name: report_intent
toolCalls.args: {"intent": "Diagnosing MTP exit code 8"}

toolCalls.name: view
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-e44fd23a277e4e6ba5da5a105633465f/TestProject.csproj"}

toolCalls.name: view
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-e44fd23a277e4e6ba5da5a105633465f/ConditionalTests.cs"}

toolResult.sessionLog: Diagnosing MTP exit code 8

index 0000000..0000000 100644

+++ b/tmp/sv-e44fd23a277e4e6ba5da5a105633465f/TestProject.csproj
@@ -1,12 +1,12 @@
 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

data.content:
**Exit code 8** means "no tests were found." MTP treats zero discovered tests as an error by default, unlike VSTest which returned success.

The fix is a single MSBuild property:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to exit successfully -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the issue. The project uses `EnableMSTestRunner` (MTP) and all tests are behind `#if INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS`. When that define isn't set, zero tests are discovered, and MTP returns exit code 8 (no tests found), which fails CI.

The fix is to set the `<TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>` property in the project file. This tells MTP to return exit code 0 instead of 8 when no tests are discovered.

Let me apply this fix.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to exit successfully -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-e44fd23a277e4e6ba5da5a105633465f/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>", "new_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to exit successfully -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>"}

data.reasoningText:
The property doesn't seem to be taking effect. Let me check the exact property name - it might be different. Let me look up what the correct property name is for this behavior.

Actually, looking at this more carefully, the `dotnet test` command might be wrapping the MTP runner. The exit code from the test host is 8, but `dotnet test` translates it. Let me check the actual test log and also verify the property name.

The property might be `--ignore-exit-code` on CLI, or a different MSBuild property. Let me check.

Actually, looking at this more carefully, the MSBuild property I used should be correct: `TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests`. 

But maybe the property name is slightly different or this version of MSTest doesn't support it yet. Looking at the output showing "Failed! - Failed: 0, Passed: 0, Skipped: 0, Total: 0" followed by "error run failed", it seems like the property either isn't being recognized or I need to verify the exact name against the MSTest documentation.

Actually, I'm realizing the issue might be different — the exit code 1 from `dotnet test` is a wrapper around the underlying test host, which is actually returning exit code 8. Let me check the log file to see what's really happening underneath.

data.content: That's the MSBuild property to pass CLI arguments to the MTP runner. Let me update the project file with the correct approach:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to exit successfully -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Return exit code 0 when no tests are discovered (e.g. conditional compilation) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to exit successfully -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Return exit code 0 when no tests are discovered (e.g. conditional compilation) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**The fix:** MTP exit code 8 means "no tests discovered," which is an error by default (unlike VSTest). Add this to your `.csproj`:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

This passes `--ignore-exit-code 8` to the MTP runner, making it return 0 instead of 8 when zero tests are found. Actual test failures still report non-zero. If you have multiple projects affected, put this in a `Directory.Build.props` instead.
